english: region or part of the world? gaṅ-gi dus (gang gi dus) which time? in the latter case it may also mean whose: gaṅ-gi lam (gang gi lam) whose way? p̉yi naṅ rnyis čós lugs gaṅ bzaṅ (phyi nang rnyis chos lugs gang bzang) which of the two doctrines, the Brahmanic or the Buddhist, is the right one? p̉yogs gaṅ-nas oṅ (phyogs gang nas ong), ṅo mi śes-pas (ngo mi shes pas) not knowing from what part of the country she comes Glr.; ma ni gaṅ yin bu ni gaṅ yin bye-brag p̉yes (ma ni gang yin bu ni gang yin bye brag phyes) decide which is the mother and which the child Dzl.; gaṅ że-na (gang zhe na) lit. 'if one asks which?' corresponds sometimes to the English 'namely, to wit, viz.'; gaṅ-na (gang na) where? gaṅ-la (gang la) whither? gaṅ-nas (gang nas), gaṅ-las (gang las) whence? gaṅ-du (gang du) where? whither? gaṅ-na-ba (gang na ba) = ga-na-ba (ga na ba) v. above; gaṅ-pa (gang pa), yul gaṅ-pa (yul gang pa), col. *gaṅ-yul-pa* (*gang yul pa*), from what country? — 2. C. for ċi (ci) what? *ghaṅ zér-ra(m)* (*ghang zer ra(m)*) what shall I say? *k̉yó-kyi miṅ-la ghaṅ zér-ghyi yó-dham* (*khyo kyi ming la ghang zer gyi yo dham*) what is your name? *gháṅ-la yoṅ* (*ghang la yong*) what are you coming for? what do you want? — 2. rel., or rather correl. pron., who, which, he who, she who, whoever, whichever, whatever, ὅστις: gaṅ p̉yir ʿoṅ-ba de ni (gang phyir 'ong ba de ni) she who follows Dzl.; gaṅ gos ʿdód-pa-la gos byuṅ (gang gos 'dod pa la gos byung) whoever wanted clothes, to him they were given Dzl.; rig-pa gaṅ rnó-ba ċig-la stér-ro (rig pa gang rno ba cig la ster ro) I give it to him who is the sharpest as to sagacity Glr.; kyód-kyi dpá-ba gaṅ yin-pa-la ḳó-bo mgu (khyod kyi dpa' ba gang yin pa la kho bo mgu) the bravery which you have shown pleases me Tar. 21, 13; rgyál-bu gaṅ-du tṣe p̉ós-pai rnás-su sóṅ-ṅo (rgyal bu gang du tshe phos pai rnas su song ngo) they went to the place where the prince had changed life Dzl.; gaṅ-gi lam sṅón-du grub-pa des (gang gi lam sngon du grub pa des) ... he whose way (of sanctification) will be completed first, shall ... Stg. Often ťams-ċád (thams cad) or a plural-sign accompanies the partic.: gaṅ mi śés-pa-dag (gang mi shes pa dag) they who do not understand Dzl. Rarely in B., but frq. in the col. language of W., the pa (pa) after the verb is supplied by a gerundial particle, such as na (na), nas (nas): *gaṅ táṅ-na k̉yad med* (*gang tang na khyad med*) which you intend to give is all the same. Sometimes, however, particularly in more modern literature, no pa (pa) is added to the verb at all, esp. when gaṅ (gang) is joined with yin (yin), yod (yod), or dug (dug), so that such sentences in their form are very similar to the relative sentences of occidental languages; but that this omission of pa (pa), although sanctioned by long continued use, is after all an incorrect breviloquence, and that pa (pa) must always be understood, appears from the frq. occurrence of the plural sign immediately after yod (yod) etc.: de rnyis-kyi srid gaṅ yód-rnams (de rnyis kyi srid gang yod rnams) the claims to government which both of these maintained Glr.; gaṅ-ťse (gang tshe) — déi-ťse (de'i tshe) when — then; gaṅ żig (gang zhig) whoever, if any body etc. frq.; vulgo in W. often pleon. = any or some, *gaṅ żig ťims-si p̉i-la* (*gang zhig thims si phi la*) on account of some law-suit, instead of ťims żig-gi p̉yir (thims zhig gi phyir); gaṅ lu-lá żig (gang lu la zhig) is of a similar meaning, but less frq. The import of the word is still more generalized by yaṅ (yang) being added to gaṅ (gang) or to the verb: dṅós-po gaṅ mťoṅ yaṅ (dngos po gang mthong yang) Mil. whatever he sets his eyes upon; gaṅ ltár-na yaṅ (gang ltar na yang), gaṅ yin kyaṅ (gang yin kyang) whatsoever it may be, however that may be, be that as it may, at all events, esp. C.; gaṅ-yaṅ-rúṅ-ba (gang yang rung ba), gaṅ-rúṅ (gang rung), gaṅ-ċi-yaṅ-rúṅ (gang ci yang rung) whosoever he may be, whatsoever it may be, quicunque; rnas gaṅ-yaṅ-rúṅ-ba-na (rnas gang yang rung ba na) wherever; gaṅ-nas gaṅ-du skyes kyaṅ (gang nas gang du skyes kyang) out of which class of beings and into whichsoever I shall be re-born Dzl. — 3. indefinite pron., used absolutely, each, every, any, all, when followed by a negation = not any, none, no: żo dar ču sogs gaṅ yaṅ ḳa (zho dar chu sogs gang yang kha), curdled milk, buttermilk, water, every thing tastes bitter Med.; saṅs-rgyás gaṅ-gis kyaṅ ma bċágs-pa (sangs rgyas gang gis kyang ma bcags pa) not yet trodden by any Buddha Glr.; p̉an gaṅ ťogs gyis (phan gang thogs gyis) be as useful as ever possible Mil.; gaṅ-dag (gang dag) all Glr. and elsewhere; dé-dag mi byuṅ gaṅ yaṅ med (de dag mi byung gang yang med) these are to be found everywhere; gaṅ-la gaṅ-dul (gang la gang 'dul) converting each in the manner best suited to him; gaṅ-gis kyaṅ (gang gis kyang) = ċis-kyaṅ (cis kyang) by all means; gaṅ-gis kyaṅ dgós-pa méd-pa (gang gis kyang dgos pa med pa) altogether useless Mil.; gaṅ daṅ gaṅ (gang dang gang) Cs., Sch. (more frq. gaṅ daṅ ċi (gang dang ci)) every thing whatsoever Glr.
